hero.badge: RBI-licensed · PCI-DSS Level 1 · Made in Bengaluru
hero.subtitle: UPI, cards, NetBanking, wallets, SEPA, ACH — 135 currencies, one integration. When a card fails at HDFC, Payze re-routes through ICICI, RuPay, then UPI. Your customer never notices. You don't lose the sale.

trustStrip.label: Live across 47+ teams · ₹18.4 Cr processed today

dashboardPreview.title: The dashboard your finance team will actually open.
dashboardPreview.subtitle: Auto-surfaced intelligence every morning. Platform health, compliance attention, and a live read of your volume — without building the report yourself.

features.title:
Everything an Indian payments stack needs,
nothing your engineering team has to wire up.

items.title: Intelligent routing
items.desc: Card declined at HDFC? Payze tries ICICI, then RuPay, then UPI. The model learns per-bank, per-BIN, per-hour.

items.title: UPI Autopay & NACH
items.desc: Native support for every Indian recurring rail. Mandate onboarding that actually completes — 94% success vs industry 72%.

items.title: Fraud that stays quiet
items.desc: ML trained on 180M+ Indian transactions. False-positive rate 4× lower than rule-based systems. Dispute win rate 2.3× industry average.

items.title: Collect from anywhere
items.desc: 135 currencies, every major local method — UPI, cards, SEPA, ACH, Pix, Alipay, FPX. Unified in INR in your dashboard.

items.title: One-day integration
items.desc: Node, Python, PHP, Go, Ruby SDKs. Webhooks with retry & signing. Test in test mode, flip a toggle, you're live.

items.title: Reports that explain themselves
items.desc: Every anomaly described in plain English. Reconciliation reports auto-emailed. GST-ready exports.

developers.title: A clean API your team will actually like.
developers.subtitle: Typed SDKs, signed webhooks, test-mode parity. The kind of surface area you can stand up in an afternoon and sleep well behind at scale.

tabs.label: Node.js
Python
tabs.code:
import payze

client = payze.Client(api_key=os.environ['PAYZE_KEY'])

# Create a payment link
link = client.collect.create(
    amount=24999,            # paise — ₹249.99
    currency='INR',
    customer={'email': 'priya@studio.com'},
    reference='INV-2026-00842',
    allow_methods=['upi', 'cards', 'netbanking']
)

print(link.url)
# → https://pay.payze.com/rYo72a

bullets.title: Typed SDKs
bullets.desc: Node · Python · PHP · Go · Ruby · .NET · Java — first-class types, IDE autocomplete, semver contracts.

bullets.title: Signed webhooks
bullets.desc: HMAC-signed with automatic retry, exponential backoff, replay protection out of the box.

bullets.title: Test = production
bullets.desc: Test mode is a bit-for-bit mirror of live. No surprise schema changes when you flip the toggle.

security.title:
Built for Indian regulations,
validated against global standards.
security.subtitle: Payze operates under RBI's Payment Aggregator licence with the full control stack you'd expect from a tier-1 payments provider.

items.quote: We were losing 14% of checkouts to card declines we couldn't explain. Four weeks in on Payze, that number is 3.8%. Routing works, integration was a day, reconciliation reports now arrive on their own.
items.name: Vikram Kothari

items.quote: The cross-border piece is what sold us — one dashboard for INR, USD, EUR, and SGD collections with FEMA reporting done by default. It saved us three months of compliance work.
items.name: Meera Subramanian

items.quote: Our engineering team integrated Payze in a single afternoon. Test-mode parity is real — what we tested on Friday ran identically in production Monday. SDK types are strict, webhooks are reliable. Refreshing.
items.name: Rohan Krishnan
